behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model judgment quality: whether explicit imagery is actually classified as unsafe. A unit/integration test with stubbed model output cannot reveal misclassification by the real vision model (MOCK-HIDE + LLM-DEP). Rejected Workflow: behavior text describes one moderation decision path for one submission, not a multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a labeled explicit image to the real vision moderation model in the moderation service path and assert the persisted decision is `held_for_review` and publish action is not executed.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Core risk is robustness of real text-model understanding under adversarial obfuscation; deterministic mocks will always pass if preconfigured and hide real failures (LLM-DEP + MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: this is a single classification requirement, not multi-step agentic orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants (leet substitutions, homoglyphs, spacing/punctuation tricks) through the production text moderation model path and assert each is flagged and routed to human review.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module handoff invariant (image result + text result + decision/publish path). Real AI quality is not under test; behavior is conditional on already passing checks. In-process fakes for both classifiers are sufficient to surface routing/state bugs (MOCK-MISS). Integration brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no dependence on real model output content, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation orchestrator, decision logic, and persistence/publish modules with classifier stubs returning `pass` for both modalities; assert exactly one publish record/event is created and no hold/drop state is stored.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly spans multiple model-backed steps in sequence plus final decision, and correctness is end-to-end latency under real execution characteristics. Stubs/fakes can hide real latency variance and queuing effects from model calls and orchestration (REAL-INFRA + LLM-involved MULTI-STEP). Rejected lower levels: text says 'full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision'.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through the deployed pipeline using real image and text model calls, measure submission-to-decision latency, and assert p95 (or required percentile) is <=5s during peak-load profile.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is fallback orchestration/state transition on dependency failure: unavailable -> enqueue retry -> no auto-approve. This can be reproduced with a deterministic failureing model client stub and real queue module wiring. Integration brake: no mandatory real OS/network behavior in the behavior text, no dependence on model output quality, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run moderation service with vision-client stub returning `unavailable` errors; assert image post enters retry queue with retry metadata and no publish/approve action occurs.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: This is consistency across repeated model-involved decisions over time, not a single call. Real failures often emerge from non-deterministic model outputs and state/caching handoff across submissions, which mocks hide. Multiple agentic evaluations in sequence are inherent to the behavior (MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP). Rejected Agentic-only: behavior text requires repeated submissions 'multiple times within a short window,' i.e., sequential pipeline behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the defined window through the real moderation path, then assert decision stability according to policy (e.g., identical final state across attempts or deterministic cache-governed outcome).
